stemHtml: On a multi-engine piston aircraft, one of the features of a pressure-fed fuel system is the capability to do what?

options.a: To ensure continuous flow to the engines, without the ability to isolate fuel leaks.
options.b: To feed fuel from any fuel tank to any engine.
options.c: To automatically balance fuel between tanks in case of engine failure.
options.d: To use a small amount of unusable fuel for the purposes of cooling essential engine components.
